location.office_time_required: four days a week in the office for those near one of our offices; Fridays from home; fully remote program also available

secondary_archetype: BizOps / Strategy & Ops — Voice of Member / Customer Insights

level_signals: Partners with senior leaders and executives but no people management mentioned

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of experience in consulting, product management, product operations, strategy & operations, user research, customer insights, analytics, or similar roles that required structured problem solving and cross-functional influence.
must_haves.domain: consulting / product / strategy / analytics / customer insights

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A track record of turning complex information into clear, compelling narratives that help others understand what matters and what to do next.

Strong analytical instincts and comfort working with both quantitative and qualitative data. You know how to ask thoughtful questions, separate signal from noise, and build confidence in your conclusions.

A high degree of curiosity, ownership, and self-motivation. You're energized by ambiguous problems, proactively explore new ideas, and don't wait for someone else to tell you what to investigate next.

Experience using AI tools to learn quickly, investigate problems, automate workflows, or improve how work gets done. You don't need to be an engineer, but you're excited to experiment and continuously expand your toolkit.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor your message for audiences ranging from individual contributors to senior leaders and executives.
A genuine curiosity about what drives member experiences, paired with a desire to influence decisions that create meaningful impact at scale.

scope.surface: Voice of the Member team — member sentiment, feedback, and experience insights across Chime
primary_metrics_inferred: Coverage and quality of member insights surfaced to leadership
Speed of insight delivery through automation and AI workflows
Reduction in sources of member frustration
core_responsibilities: Investigate biggest sources of member frustration and delight across Chime by combining signals from feedback, support conversations, social channels, app reviews, and operational data
Build and evolve tools, automations, and AI-enabled workflows to help teams access and act on member insights
Identify opportunities to improve manual analysis, reporting, or decision-making through automation and experimentation
Turn complex findings into clear, compelling narratives for product teams, business leaders, and executives
Lead deep-dive analyses on important member experience topics, identifying root causes and quantifying impact
Shape and deliver company-wide sentiment readouts
Proactively investigate emerging issues and surface insights

green_flags: Fully remote program explicitly mentioned alongside hybrid option
Equity and comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Role has clear ownership area (Voice of the Member team) and defined deliverables
Generous parental leave (up to 24 weeks for birthing parents)
Strong member-mission framing provides clear north star for the role
red_flags: Title is 'Program Manager' but responsibilities skew heavily toward insights, analytics, and strategy — could cause role ambiguity or title mismatch for some candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere in the JD

Location policy is ambiguous: JD is tagged #LI-Remote but benefits section describes a four-day-a-week in-office policy for those near offices — two different tracks coexist and the candidate's eligibility for each is unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years required; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from #LI-Remote tag and 'fully remote program' mention)
ambiguities: Location type: JD is tagged #LI-Remote and mentions a 'fully remote program,' but the benefits section describes a default of four days a week in the office with Fridays from home for those near an office. Two distinct work tracks appear to exist simultaneously. Treated as remote-us eligible but flagged.

Role archetype: Title is 'Program Manager' but the responsibilities and required skills (customer insights, sentiment analysis, AI-enabled workflows, narrative storytelling) more closely resemble a Strategy & Ops or Customer Insights lead. Archetype confidence set to medium.
